/ Creative Practice

Live Execution / Creative Practice Akara × FUBU / 2026

Final Output / GPT Image 2 / 16:9 / 1K / High Quality

One model. Three looks.
One pipeline.

Akara styled across three FUBU reimagined garments. The model identity is locked across every output. The garment is the variable. The pipeline runs the same way each time.

A brand brings a garment. The model identity is already built. The pipeline runs and the output is campaign-ready. No studio day, no photographer, no booking.

Process / In Order

01

Model Identity Lock

Akara base reference established in Higgsfield Soul ID. Face, skin tone, bone structure, and bob locked. ChatGPT seed used to anchor identity before any generation.

02

Garment Input

FUBU Technical Utility Jacket spec: ripstop shell, tonal embroidered logo, shockcord hem, hidden zip pockets. Translated into prompt language with fabric and silhouette detail.

03

Creative Direction Reference

Giant-figure-over-city concept. Dramatic low aerial angle, city skyline below, cinematic blue sky, helicopters in distance. Camera looking up. Wind in hair. Plane in hand.

04

Generation / GPT Image 2

16:9 aspect ratio, 1K resolution, High quality. Face lock instruction included at prompt head. Ultra photorealistic, 8K, high contrast dramatic lighting, golden warm skin against cool blue city sky.

05

Video / Gen-4 In Production

Hero frame → Runway Gen-4 Video. She turns slowly. Camera pulls back. City below. Wind moves through her jacket. Cinematic motion, no action presence only.

Full Prompt / Documented

Identity lock: This model's face is locked. Reject any generation that alters her features. Use the exact face and body from the Akara reference images. Do not change skin tone, face shape, eye shape, lips, nose, jaw, or bone structure. Black female model, deep rich ebony skin, warm undertones, high cheekbones, full lips, dark almond eyes, ear-length jet black bob.

Styling: Wearing the red FUBU utility jacket cropped boxy ripstop shell with tonal red embroidered logo, shockcord hem, hidden zip pockets form fitting medium wash denim jorts, white clean sole sneakers.

Scene: Akara as a giant figure towering over a major city skyline, shot from a dramatic low aerial angle looking up at her, city buildings and streets far below, dramatic cloudy sky with cinematic blue tones, helicopters in background distance. She looks directly into camera with a calm powerful expression, one hand extended slightly toward the lens with a plane in hand, pulling it out of the sky. Wind moves through her hair.

Technical: Cinematic editorial photography style, high contrast dramatic lighting, golden warm tones on skin against cool blue city sky, ultra photorealistic, hyperdetailed, 8K, no watermark, no text.

Settings: GPT Image 2 · Aspect ratio 16:9 · Resolution 1K · Quality High

001 / FAMD 3375 and 4359 / Real Client

The Doll House

Houston, TX / Real Client / 2026

Runway MLChatGPTCanva AIClaudeGoogle GeminiSCAMPER

"A brand with strong physical identity and almost no digital presence. The goal was building a full campaign without a traditional photography budget."

Real boutique. Real brief. No photography budget.

The Doll House carries a loyal in-store community and over 65 women-owned brands. The challenge was helping a brand with a strong physical identity launch its first proprietary clothing label using AI-generated content in place of a traditional shoot.

Runway ML Workflow

A 5-node Runway ML pipeline takes a locked editorial model image and a swappable garment input, then generates a lookbook photo, ecommerce product shot, and social media content from a single pipeline run. The workflow is repeatable no photography background required. This is the campaign infrastructure the client now owns.

Runway Workflow Screenshot

Add workflow screenshot here 5-node pipeline diagram

A.H.

Outcome: 9-piece capsule collection priced $70–$325. Competitive analysis against Emerson Rose (24.3K) and Thrifted Threads (169K). Full editorial photoshoot with 5 models executed using AI-generated assets.

Collection

Editorial Shoot

002 / AI Model Development / Creative Direction

Akara

AI Model and Creative Direction / @akaracali.official

HiggsfieldChatGPTRunway Gen-4HappyHorse 1.0

A consistent AI model built and directed across multiple looks, angles, and styling variations. She does not code switch. The same person in every room.

Creative Direction Rationale

Akara was built to answer a specific question: can you hold a person's identity consistent across multiple AI tools without losing what makes them distinct? Most AI character work fails at the detail level the face shifts, the tone drifts, the energy changes between platforms. The direction for Akara was to anchor everything in specificity. California-rooted, SoCal and Central Valley culture. Rich brown skin, jet black bob, casual editorial not polished for a brand, polished for herself. The creative choice to lock her identity through ChatGPT as seed reference before moving into Runway Gen-4 meant the character survived the pipeline instead of getting generalized by it. She was the creative brief and the output at the same time.

Before / Higgsfield Soul ID

After / Runway Gen-4 and ChatGPT Seed

01

Foundation

Built on Higgsfield Soul ID. Established base identity, face, and visual consistency across multiple angles.

02

Render

Runway Gen-4 Image with enhanced prompts. ChatGPT used as seed reference to anchor exact identity.

03

Motion

Best Runway output used as starting frame for HappyHorse 1.0 video. Brought to life with natural movement and warm golden studio lighting.

003 / FAMD 4359 / Cultural Editorial

Tupac Reimagined

Spring and Fall Editorial / 2026

Runway Gen-4ChatGPTGoogle GeminiClaudeSCAMPER

A cultural fashion editorial translating Tupac Shakur's 1990s LA aesthetic into two modern looks one for Spring, one for Fall. Visual mood boarding and SCAMPER were used to make deliberate design decisions rather than direct recreations.

Reference / Original Aesthetic  ·  Spring Look / Runway Gen-4

SCAMPER Application

Substitute Leather vest replaced with a blue paisley linen vest. More sustainable, spring-appropriate, and consistent with his original blue bandana signature.

Modify Fit adjusted to be more tailored and considered rather than relaxed and oversized.

Adapt Color palette updated for Spring 2026 trends while keeping the cultural reference intact. This process pushed the work past recreation into actual design decision-making.

Spring Look / ChatGPT render via Runway Gen-4  ·  Fall Look / ChatGPT render via Runway Gen-4

004 / Ecommerce Case Study / Customer Research

Nöz Ecommerce

Customer Research / Empathy Map / Shopify Strategy

ShopifyEmpathy MappingCustomer ResearchEcommerce Strategy

"Understanding the customer is the strategy. Everything else is execution."

Overview

Nöz is a direct-to-consumer brand case study focused on building an ecommerce operation grounded in real customer insight. The work starts with research who the customer is, what they actually need, and where the current experience fails them then builds outward into Shopify architecture and product strategy.

Empathy Map

Built through customer interviews, social listening, and behavioral observation. Maps what the Nöz customer thinks, feels, says, and does and where those four things don't align. That gap is where the product and UX opportunity lives.

Empathy Map Visual

Upload empathy map image here.
Built in previous session background updated to match portfolio (#0f0f0f). Watermark: A.H.

A.H.

Shopify Architecture

Product taxonomy, collection logic, and navigation built around how the customer shops not how the brand wants to organize its catalog. Recommendations cover homepage layout, product page hierarchy, and the checkout flow.

Shopify Screenshot 01
Shopify Screenshot 02
Shopify Screenshot 03
Shopify Screenshot 04

Key Findings

01

Discovery Gap

Customers find the product through social but can't locate it on-site. Navigation and search are the first friction point.

02

Trust Signal Absence

No reviews, limited photography, and sparse product copy reduce conversion confidence especially for first-time buyers.

03

Repeat Purchase Barrier

No loyalty mechanic or post-purchase communication keeping customers in the brand's orbit after the first order.

005 / FAMD 4359 / Brand Case Study

FUBU Reimagined

Heritage to Future / 2026

Brand StrategyImpact AnalysisDesign IterationCultural ResearchChatGPTRunway MLSCAMPER

"Rooted in culture. Built for the future. From a $100K mortgage to $6B in retail sales and what happened after."

Case Study Overview

A full brand impact analysis and three-iteration design reimagination of FUBU. One of the most successful Black-owned businesses in American history studied through a social, environmental, and economic lens. Academic research translated into real design direction.

At its peak, over $350 million in annual sales. FUBU reshaped what was possible for Black entrepreneurs in fashion. Overextension through excess inventory was the breaking point by 2003, FUBU had pulled out of the U.S. market. The environmental cost was also unaccounted for at scale. A single pair of jeans requires approximately 3,781 liters of water from cultivation to shelf. At $350M+ in annual sales, this was happening at significant and unacknowledged scale.

Design Iterations

01

Modernized Heritage Tracksuit

Gray track set. Straight-leg silhouette, smaller chest branding, quarter-zip packable concept. First moment the brand started feeling technical rather than nostalgic. Side panel striping still read retro that's the limit of this iteration.

02

Fashion Week Baseball Jersey

Black tonal cropped jersey. It stopped trying to revive Y2K and started using Y2K as inspiration that distinction matters. Closest to Fear of God Athletics, Korean fashion week streetwear, and monochrome luxury sportswear.

03

Technical Utility FUBU

Cropped utility jacket. This is where the concept stopped feeling like merch and started feeling like a legitimate fashion label evolution. Cropped body, oversized sleeves, utility pockets, adjustable hems. Tiny tonal branding integrated rather than decorative. This third lane is the strongest. The inspiration became FUBU's cultural DNA rather than FUBU's old product catalog.

Strategic Recommendations

Reclaim the legacy with greater awareness. Update silhouettes with cleaner lines, minimalist typography, and repositioned logo placement. Align with culturally relevant artists the way LL Cool J connected the brand to a generation in the 1990s. Replace mass production with a limited release model. The third iteration is the strongest. Technical streetwear using FUBU's cultural DNA as the brief not the old product catalog.

002 / AI Campaign Production / 2026

Akara × FUBU

Three garments. One model. One pipeline. Campaign-ready.

Runway ML GPT Image 2 ChatGPT Seed Higgsfield Soul ID Runway Workflows

"Akara wearing FUBU. Three garments, three looks, one consistent face. Built entirely in AI without a studio or a shoot day."

The Brief

FUBU has three reimagined garments a technical utility jacket, a tonal football jersey, and a colorblock tracksuit. The goal was to show what a campaign looks like on a real, consistent model across all three pieces without a traditional production budget. Akara is the model. Runway is the studio.

The Workflow

Akara's identity was locked using three reference angles front, three-quarter, side fed into the Runway Workflow alongside each garment spec sheet. GPT Image 2 generated each campaign image with face lock instructions anchoring her features across every output. Three prompts. Three garments. Same face every time.

Runway Workflow / Akara × FUBU Pipeline / 3-node structure / 2026

Campaign Outputs

Left: Utility Jacket / Blue · Center: Football Jersey / Charcoal · Right: Tracksuit / FUBU Red + Black

01

Model Identity

Akara's face locked via ChatGPT seed + three-angle reference inputs. Deep ebony skin, jet black bob, high cheekbones consistent across every output.

02

Garment Input

FUBU spec sheets used as visual reference nodes. Fabric details, silhouette, colorway all fed directly into the generation prompt.

03

Pipeline Output

Three campaign images. Three distinct looks. One consistent model. Ready to hand to a brand team, a social strategist, or an ecommerce team.

What This Demonstrates

A brand with multiple SKUs needs campaign imagery for each one. Traditional production means separate shoot days and separate budgets. This pipeline takes a locked model and a garment spec and generates campaign-ready output in a single run. The brand gets visual consistency across every piece without a studio.
